rationale: Fundamentally unprovable. HashMap<V>.insert ensures only `self@ == old(self)@.insert(key@, value)` (constrains the abstract view); HashMap<V> is `#[verifier(external_body)]` so concrete `==` is SMT-uninterpreted. Antecedent gives post1_self_@ == post2_self_@ (via Map extensionality), but cannot bridge to post1_self_ == post2_self_. Tried structural `==` and extensional `=~=` between the wrapper values; both assert fail. r1==r2 is trivial (unit). Without forbidden axioms (assume/external_body/etc.), the determinism check is not closable. Same pattern as other ironkv HashMap-insert determinism artifacts in this batch.
